firstRunHint: First run detected. Create admin credentials.
initAdminDesc: Create the first owner account for the dashboard.
initBootstrapDesc: These bootstrap values are written to config.json during first-run setup.
initWebUIEnabledDesc: Turn this off only if you intentionally want a CLI-only setup after restart.
initRestartNotice: Startup settings are saved immediately, but the running service must restart before gateway and WebUI changes take effect.
initStorageReadonly: These paths are shown for confirmation only so you know where Nekobot is storing its runtime data during first-run setup.
initRestartRequired: Initialization complete. Restart the service to apply: {0}.
initPasswordHint: Use a password you can remember for local admin access. You can rotate it later.
initSubmittingDescription: Saving startup settings and preparing your first session. You will be redirected automatically.
initNextStepDescription: Create the first admin account, then Nekobot will sign you in and open the chat workspace.
promptContextSourcesDescription: Inspect which stable and dynamic sources will feed one request before it hits the model.
promptContextSourcesEmpty: No context preview loaded yet. Run a preview to inspect the current prompt inputs.
webhooksDescription: Configure and test the generic webhook trigger endpoint.
webhooksPathHint: Path changes are saved to config, but the live webhook route updates after service restart.
webhooksTestHint: This button uses the stable internal test endpoint and does not depend on the saved webhook path.
policyDescription: Inspect built-in policy presets and evaluate sample requests.
chatEmptyHint: Send a message to start chatting
chatEmptyDescription: Pick a route on the left, then use this workspace for faster iteration and clearer replies.
chatComposerHint: Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a new line.
marketplaceRemoteHeadline: Search the external skills registry with the configured proxy.
marketplaceSearchRemoteHint: Enter a query to search the remote registry.
marketplaceHeaderDescription: Review installed skills, inspect raw content, and toggle capabilities without leaving the dashboard.
marketplaceHeroTitle: Treat skills like runtime modules, not hidden markdown files.
marketplaceHeroDescription: Inspect runtime readiness, missing requirements, install plans, and parsed content before enabling a skill in production.
marketplaceInstallHeadline: Install from a git URL or local path without leaving the dashboard.
marketplaceInstallDescription: Remote installs clone through the same skills proxy. Local paths are copied into the writable skills directory.
marketplaceLastInstallNone: No install has been triggered in this session.
marketplaceWorkspaceHeadline: Treat the runtime workspace as a first-class dependency.
marketplaceWorkspaceDescription: Bootstrap status, daily memory log, and heartbeat state are visible here instead of hidden behind CLI setup.
marketplaceSourcesHeadline: Multi-path overrides are visible and auditable from Web.
marketplaceSourcesDescription: Later sources override earlier ones. This helps diagnose why a skill definition changed at runtime.
marketplaceNoSources: No skill sources reported by the backend.
marketplaceSnapshotsHeadline: Capture current skill state before experiments and restore it when needed.
marketplaceSnapshotsDescription: Useful before bulk installs, remote sync, or manual edits in the writable skill directory.
marketplaceRetentionPolicy: Retention policy keeps the newest {0} snapshots. Use prune to apply it immediately.
marketplaceRetentionUnset: Snapshot retention limit is not configured yet.
marketplaceNoSnapshots: No snapshots yet. Create one before the next large skill change.
marketplaceSelectSkillDescription: Choose a skill from the left list to inspect metadata, body content, and runtime status.
marketplaceRuntimeReadyDescription: This skill passes the current runtime checks and can be enabled immediately.
marketplaceRuntimeBlockedDescription: This skill is blocked by missing requirements in the current runtime environment.
marketplaceDependencyDescription: Review the install steps parsed from skill metadata and run them from the dashboard when needed.
marketplaceNoInstallSteps: No install steps declared for this skill.
systemRuntimePathsDescription: Use this to confirm which config file, runtime DB, and workspace the current process is pointing at.
systemTasksHeadline: Background agent work is visible here.
systemTasksDescription: Use this panel to spot pending, running, and failed background work before it becomes invisible control-plane state.
systemTasksEmpty: No background tasks have been recorded yet.
systemSessionRuntimeHeadline: Approval and pending session state is visible here.
systemSessionRuntimeDescription: Use this panel to spot chat sessions currently held by manual approval or waiting on a pending tool action.
systemSessionRuntimeEmpty: No session-level runtime state is currently tracked.
systemAgentDefinitionDescription: This is the compatibility snapshot derived from current runtime config. It marks the boundary between route defaults, permission mode, tool policy, and prompt sections.
systemAgentDefinitionEmpty: No agent definition snapshot is available.
systemRawStatusHeadline: Full status payload for deeper inspection.
systemQMDDescription: This reflects actual runtime availability, not just saved config.
permissionRulesPageDescription: Define persistent tool permission rules before approval-mode fallback. Match by tool name and optionally narrow by session or runtime.
permissionRulesHeroTitle: Make tool execution policy explicit instead of hiding it behind a single global approval mode.
permissionRulesHeroDescription: Use allow, deny, or ask rules per tool. Narrow rules to a session or runtime when one workflow needs stricter control than the rest of the system.
permissionRulesEmpty: No permission rules yet. Create one to override approval mode for a tool.
permissionRulesEditorDescription: Higher priority wins first. When priorities tie, narrower scopes beat broader rules.
permissionRulesEnabledHint: Disabled rules stay stored but never match.
permissionRulesPreviewAllow: Matching tool calls bypass approval fallback and execute immediately.
permissionRulesPreviewDeny: Matching tool calls are rejected before the tool runs.
permissionRulesPreviewAsk: Matching tool calls always enter the pending approval queue, even if the current mode is auto.
chatRouteHeadline: Shape how this conversation is routed before you send.
chatActualRouteHint: This shows the provider/model the backend actually used after pool expansion, fallback, and cooldown checks.
chatRuntimeControlsRoute: This runtime controls provider, model, and prompt routing for the turn.
chatRuntimeLoadFailedDescription: The runtime selector cannot trust stale topology data. Fix this before routing a turn to a specific runtime.
chatRuntimeLoadFailedDetailFallback: Runtime topology data is currently unavailable.
chatRuntimeEmptyHint: No websocket runtime binding is configured yet. Auto route will use the default chat path.
chatRuntimeUnavailableHint: Runtime bindings exist, but none are currently active. Re-enable the bound account, runtime, or binding in Runtime Topology.
chatProvidersLoadFailedDescription: The route panel needs fresh provider data before it can safely shape provider and model selection.
chatPromptsLoadFailedDescription: Prompt bindings are session-scoped. The prompt picker stays blocked until the latest prompt set is available.
chatHarnessAuditDescription: Inspect tool runs, failures, and session-linked activity without leaving the dashboard.
chatHarnessWatchDescription: Jump to config to adjust watch patterns, debounce, and runtime behavior.
chatFileMentionsSummary: Expanded {0} file reference(s) for this turn.
harnessAuditDescription: Review harness activity, recent tool executions, and watch-driven runs in one place.
harnessAuditConsoleDescription: Recent harness events are grouped here for quick debugging and workflow review.
harnessAuditClearConfirm: Clear the harness audit log? This removes the current JSONL history.
harnessAuditEmptyDescription: Run watch, undo, or tool-backed chat actions to populate the audit stream.
watchRuntimeDescription: This is the latest watcher execution reported by the backend.
chatCustomModelHint: Leave empty to follow the selected model.
chatFallbackSelectHint: Select providers or pools in the order the backend should try them after the primary route.
chatTranscriptSubtitle: A cleaner workspace for testing prompts, providers, and models.
chatSystemPromptsHint: Applied as instruction-level overlays before the request is sent.
chatUserPromptsHint: Prepended to the user message as reusable prompt templates.
chatPromptEmpty: No enabled prompts available yet. Create some in the Prompts page first.
chatNoProvidersDescription: Chat requires at least one provider to handle requests. Add a provider first, then come back here to test.
chatNoPromptsDescription: Prompts let you inject system instructions or reusable templates. Create one and enable it to enhance your chats.
launchCommandHelp: Leave empty to run the selected tool without extra args.
noToolSessionsHint: Tool sessions run terminal commands and long-lived processes. Create one to get started.
splitRequiresAnotherSession: Split view needs two different sessions. Open another session first.
killConfirmDescription: Are you sure you want to kill this process? This action cannot be undone.
toolSocketError: Terminal WS disconnected, switched to polling
accessNotAvailable: No access credentials available for this session
proxyUrlRequired: Proxy URL is required when proxy mode is custom
externalAccessDisabled: External access is disabled for this session
otpUnavailable: Unable to generate one-time password right now
providersHeaderDescription: Add endpoints, verify readiness, and keep routing defaults visible while you shape provider pools.
providersHeroTitle: Build a clean provider graph before you touch routing.
providersHeroDescription: Configure each provider once, keep model discovery close to the record, and make the active default obvious.
providersNoMatchDescription: Try a broader keyword or clear the search box.
providersEmptyDescription: Start with one provider that can successfully list models. Once that works, route defaults and model switching become much easier to reason about.
providerGroupsTitle: Compose rotation pools without dropping into raw config.
providerGroupsDescription: Pools are saved to agents.defaults.provider_groups and the backend expands them during routing and fallback execution.
providerGroupsEmptyDescription: Create a pool when you want the backend to rotate between multiple providers with one route name.
providerGroupsSaveHint: Saving writes the pool list back into agents.defaults.provider_groups.
providerGroupsOverviewTitle: Keep routing building blocks visible while editing providers.
providerGroupsOverviewEmpty: Saved provider pools will appear here once you add them.
providerGroupsConfigUnavailable: Agent defaults are not available yet.
providerGroupValidationNameRequired: Each provider pool needs a name.
providerGroupValidationMembersMin: Provider pool "{0}" needs at least two members.
providerGroupStrategyRoundRobinDescripti: Rotate providers in a stable order.
providerGroupStrategyLeastUsedDescriptio: Prefer the provider with the lowest request count.
providerPanelModelsHint: Discover or add models before using this provider in chat.
providerRuntimeIdle: No runtime failures recorded in memory.
providerRuntimeHealthy: No active cooldown. Backend will consider this provider available.
cronDeleteConfirmDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this cron job? This action cannot be undone.
snapshotDeleteConfirmDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this snapshot? This action cannot be undone.
providerDraftUpdated: Provider draft updated. Click Save to persist.
discoveryFailed: Discovery failed: {0}. You can still add models manually.
deleteConfirmDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this provider? This action cannot be undone.
wechatBindingDescription: Start QR binding from the web dashboard and manage multiple stored accounts.
wechatNoQr: Click "Start Binding" to generate a QR code.
wechatMultiAccountHint: WeChat can keep multiple bound accounts. You can switch the active account or remove an old one without clearing all credentials.
promptLibraryTitle: Manage reusable prompts as first-class runtime assets.
promptLibraryDescription: Store system instructions and user templates once, then bind them globally, by channel, or per chat session.
promptEmpty: No prompts yet. Create one to start building reusable chat recipes.
promptEditorDescription: Use system mode for instruction overlays and user mode for reusable input templates.
promptEnabledHint: Disabled prompts stay in storage but are skipped during resolution.
promptBindingsDescription: Global bindings affect every run, channel bindings target one channel type, and session bindings override a single chat session.
promptBindingEnabledHint: Disabled bindings remain stored but are ignored at runtime.
promptBindingsEmpty: No bindings yet. Add one to make prompts active.
promptEmptyGuide: No prompts yet. Create a prompt first, then bind it to make it active in chat.
promptBindingsEmptyGuide: No bindings configured. Prompts only become active after binding them here.
promptWorkflowHint: Workflow: 1. Create a prompt  2. Bind it (global, channel, or session)  3. Use it in Chat
configRestartRequired: Saved. Restart the service to apply: {0}.
systemServiceHeadline: Restart the installed gateway service from Web.
systemServiceDescription: Use this after editing startup-bound settings so the running process rebinds to the saved bootstrap config.
systemServiceNotInstalledHint: The gateway service is not installed on this host yet, so Web can report status but cannot restart it.
systemServiceReloadHint: Reload updates the running process with saved runtime config when a full service restart is unnecessary.
systemServiceRestartHint: The service is installed but not currently running. Restart will ask the service manager to re-launch it with the saved startup arguments.
configSectionDescAgents: Agent defaults, model routing, workspace and MCP integration.
agentsRoutingHeadline: Configure the default route the backend should execute.
agentsRoutingDescription: Set the global provider or provider pool, default model, and backend fallback order without editing raw config.
agentsDefaultProviderHint: Choose a provider or provider pool as the global default route.
agentsDefaultModelHint: Pick the default model used when the route does not override it.
agentsFallbackProvidersHint: The backend tries these providers or pools in order when the primary route fails.
agentsRuntimeDescription: Core execution settings that still matter after the route is chosen.
configSectionDescGateway: Gateway listen host and service ports.
configSectionDescTools: Web tool behavior, exec timeout and sandbox settings.
configSectionDescTranscription: Speech-to-text provider, API base, model and timeout.
configSectionDescMemory: Long-term, semantic and episodic memory controls.
configSectionDescSessions: Control which sessions are persisted and how much conversation detail is recorded.
memoryBasicHeadline: Persistent memory injected into the prompt
memoryBasicDescription: This layer controls whether nekobot keeps file-backed memory and where that memory is stored.
memoryEnabledHint: Turns persistent memory on or off for the agent runtime.
memoryContextEnabledHint: Controls whether file-backed memory is inserted into the system prompt.
memoryContextIncludeWorkspaceHint: Read the workspace root MEMORY.md file into prompt context.
memoryContextIncludeLongTermHint: Read the persistent memory store into prompt context.
memoryContextRecentDaysHint: How many recent daily notes should be included in prompt context.
memoryContextMaxCharsHint: Hard cap for injected memory text before truncation.
memoryBackendHint: Choose where persistent memory sections are stored.
memoryFilePathHint: Used when backend is file. Leave empty to use workspace/memory.
memoryKVPrefixHint: Prefix for key-value backed memory sections.
memoryPromptHint: Prompt context now pulls from workspace/MEMORY.md, long-term memory, and today's notes. Keep this layer simple and stable.
memoryNoMigrationHint: This project is still in active development. Memory refactors optimize for the new design and do not preserve old compatibility paths.
memoryRetrievalDescription: Use these options when you want retrieval to augment the basic memory files instead of replacing them.
memorySemanticEnabledHint: Search indexed memory by vector or hybrid similarity.
memoryEpisodicEnabledHint: Keep rolling summaries for longer-running conversations.
memorySearchPolicyHint: Hybrid blends similarity and keyword matching.
memoryIncludeScoresHint: Expose retrieval scores in semantic results when supported.
memoryShortTermLimitHint: How many raw messages stay available before summaries and retrieval take over.
memoryQMDHeadline: Markdown collections as an optional retrieval layer
memoryQMDDescription: QMD is treated as an enhancement on top of basic memory, not a replacement for it.
memoryQMDEnabledHint: Turn on Query Markdown indexing and retrieval integration.
memoryQMDIncludeDefaultHint: Index the workspace memory directory as the default QMD collection.
memoryQMDSessionsEnabledHint: Continuously export chat/session artifacts into a QMD-readable directory.
memoryQMDOnBootHint: Run QMD update when nekobot starts.
memoryQMDCommandHint: Executable used to run QMD operations.
memoryQMDPathsHint: One collection per line using name|path|pattern, for example docs|/workspace/docs|**/*.md.
memoryQMDRuntimeDescription: See whether the configured QMD command is actually available and update collections without leaving the config flow.
memoryQMDCollectionsEmpty: No QMD collections have been initialized yet.
memoryQMDSessionCleanupHint: QMD session exports are persisted markdown files. Retention controls automatic cleanup, and this action applies the policy immediately.
sessionsPersistenceHeadline: Decide which chats consume disk space
sessionsPersistenceDescription: Persist only the session sources and message classes you actually need for recovery or audit.
sessionsEnabledHint: Turn conversation/session storage on or off globally.
sessionsSourcesHint: Choose which entry points are allowed to create on-disk session records.
sessionsSourceCLIDesc: Persist one-shot and interactive CLI conversations.
sessionsSourceWebUIDesc: Persist chats started from the dashboard playground.
sessionsSourceChannelsDesc: Persist conversations from Telegram, WeChat and other messaging channels.
sessionsSourceHeartbeatDesc: Persist autonomous heartbeat runs. Usually noisy and safe to disable.
sessionsSourceGatewayDesc: Persist sessions from external WebSocket gateway clients.
sessionsContentHint: Trim stored detail to reduce disk usage and limit sensitive trace retention.
sessionsContentSystemMessagesDesc: Store system-level or internal messages when they appear.
sessionsContentToolCallsDesc: Store assistant-issued tool invocations and arguments.
sessionsCleanupHint: Delete stale session records in the background so disk usage stays bounded over time.
sessionsCleanupEnabledDesc: Run a background cleanup task that prunes old persisted sessions.
sessionsCleanupIntervalMinutesDesc: How often the cleanup worker scans persisted sessions.
sessionsCleanupMaxAgeDaysDesc: Persisted sessions older than this are deleted automatically.
sessionsDiskHint: Disable noisy sources or detailed tool traces if disk growth matters more than replay fidelity.
configSectionDescHeartbeat: Autonomous heartbeat interval and task cadence.
configSectionDescApproval: Allow or deny policy and approval mode.
configSectionDescLogger: Logging level, output target and rotation strategy.
configSectionDescWebUI: Dashboard port and interactive session settings.
webuiRuntimeHeadline: Control which web-side records are kept and how quickly they are cleaned up.
webuiRuntimeDescription: Keep only the operational history you actually need. Runtime diagnostics should stay ephemeral by default.
webuiPublicBaseUrlDesc: Used when the dashboard generates external access links.
webuiToolSessionOTPSecondsDesc: One-time password lifetime in seconds for shared tool sessions.
webuiToolSessionEventsDesc: These are persisted timeline events for tool sessions. Disable them if you do not need event history.
webuiToolSessionEventsEnabledDesc: When disabled, new tool session event records are not written to disk.
webuiToolSessionEventsRetentionDaysDesc: Old tool session events older than this window are cleaned automatically.
webuiSkillSnapshotsDesc: Snapshots are useful during large marketplace changes, but they should not grow without limit.
webuiSkillSnapshotsAutoPruneDesc: Automatically delete the oldest snapshots after the configured maximum count is exceeded.
webuiSkillSnapshotsMaxCountDesc: Only the newest snapshots up to this count are kept automatically.
webuiSkillVersionsDesc: Skill change history is useful for auditing and debugging, but it should remain bounded.
webuiSkillVersionsEnabledDesc: When disabled, new skill discovery changes are no longer written to version history files.
webuiSkillVersionsMaxCountDesc: Only the newest version records up to this count are kept for each skill.
webuiSkillVersionsPruned: Pruned skill version history to {0} record(s) per skill.
webuiDiskHint: Rule of thumb: config and credentials must persist; diagnostics, temporary routing decisions, and transient runtime state should not.
configSectionHint: Editing {0}. This section is persisted to database.
configSectionHintBootstrap: Editing {0}. This startup section is persisted to config.json and usually needs restart to take effect.
configSectionHintStorage: Editing Storage. Saving this section updates config.json, migrates the runtime DB to the new directory, and still requires restart.
configPageDescription: Database-backed runtime settings. Drafts stay local until saved.
configJsonFieldHint: JSON field. Edit this section in JSON mode to preserve the structure.
configJsonModeHint: JSON mode edits the entire section exactly as stored. Use this for arrays of objects and advanced nested structures.
configNoEditableFields: This section has no editable fields.
configLoadFailedDescription: The latest config could not be loaded. The form is blocked so a load failure cannot be mistaken for empty settings.
configLoadFailedDetailFallback: Config request failed. Retry after the backend is reachable again.
noProvidersHint: No providers configured yet. Click "New" to add one.
newPasswordHint: Leave blank to keep current password.
oldPasswordRequired: Current password is required to change password.
cronPageDescription: Create and manage scheduled agent jobs
cronFallbackHint: Choose providers or pools in the order the backend should try them for this scheduled task.
cronNoProvidersWarning: No providers configured. Cron jobs need at least one provider to execute. Add a provider in the Providers page or configure routing defaults in Config.
cronEmptyJobsGuide: Create your first cron job above. You can schedule prompts to run on a recurring basis.
sessionsPageDescription: Browse, inspect, annotate, and delete chat sessions
sessionsEmptyHint: Start a conversation from Chat to create your first session.
sessionSelectHint: Select a session from the left list
marketplaceEmptyDescription: No marketplace skills are available right now.
channelInstancesDescription: Active channel runtime instances built from channel accounts or legacy config.
channelsEmptyDescription: No channels match the current filter. Switch to the All tab to see all available channels.
channelTestConfigured: {0} is configured, but this channel does not expose a runtime health probe yet.
channelFormDescription: Configure the channel parameters below. Toggle Enabled to activate.
channelFieldPasswordHint: Enter value (leave blank to keep unchanged)
channelNoFields: No configurable fields for this channel.
configSectionDescStorage: Runtime database directory and local storage paths.
storageRuntimeHeadline: Move the unified runtime database deliberately.
storageRuntimeDescription: Changing the database directory now migrates the runtime DB file first, then saves the new bootstrap path.
storageDatabaseDirDesc: Target directory for nekobot.db after the move.
storageMigrationHint: Saving this section copies the current runtime DB to the new directory and still requires a service restart before the running process uses it.
storageMigrationDetail: Use an empty or dedicated target directory. Existing non-empty runtime DB targets are rejected to avoid overwriting unrelated data.
configSectionDescRedis: Shared Redis connection used by state and bus backends.
configSectionDescState: Key-value state backend selection and file/redis prefix settings.
configSectionDescBus: Message bus backend selection and Redis bus prefix.
configSectionDescAudit: Tool call audit logging, retention policies and arg length limits.
configSectionDescUndo: Turn-level undo snapshots, max turns and persistence settings.
configSectionDescPreprocess: File mention expansion, size limits and inline file injection.
configSectionDescLearnings: Durable context compression, decay rates and summarization.
configSectionDescWatch: File system watcher, glob patterns and debounce settings.
watchEnabledHint: Run watch commands automatically when matching files change.
watchDebounceMsHint: Wait this long after a change before running the command.
watchPatternsHint: Each pattern maps a file glob to the command that should run.
watchPatternGlobHint: Files matching this glob trigger the command.
watchPatternCommandHint: Command to run after matching file changes.
watchPatternFailCommandHint: Optional fallback command to run if the main command fails.
runtimeTopologyDescription: Manage agent runtimes, channel accounts, and binding relationships in the new multi-runtime architecture.
runtimeTopologyControlDescription: This page now acts as the control plane for runtime topology. Create transport accounts, attach runtimes, and keep the routing graph editable while the backend runtime model continues to evolve.
runtimeTopologyDeleteDescription: Delete "{0}"? This change is applied immediately.
runtimeTopologyDeleteRuntimeDescription: Delete runtime "{0}"? This also removes {1} related binding(s) immediately.
runtimeTopologyDeleteAccountDescription: Delete account "{0}"? This also removes {1} related binding(s) immediately.
runtimeTopologyDeleteBindingDescription: Delete binding "{0}"? This change is applied immediately.
runtimeTopologyBindingGuard: Create at least one runtime and one channel account before defining bindings.
runtimeTopologyBindingReady: Runtimes and channel accounts are ready. You can now define routing bindings directly from this page.
runtimeTopologyRuntimeSectionDescription: Independent runtime definitions that will later own provider, prompt, harness, and memory policy.
runtimeTopologyAccountSectionDescription: Channel-agnostic transport endpoints. This layer is shared by all channels, not just WeChat.
runtimeTopologyBindingSectionDescription: Routing contracts from channel accounts to agent runtimes. Round 1 keeps this layer observable before runtime dispatch migration.
runtimeTopologyNoRuntimes: No agent runtimes have been defined yet.
runtimeTopologyNoAccounts: No channel accounts have been defined yet.
runtimeTopologyNoBindings: No account bindings have been defined yet.
runtimeTopologyLoadFailedDescription: Runtime topology data is unavailable right now. The page is blocked so a fetch failure cannot be mistaken for an empty topology.
runtimeTopologyLoadFailedDetailFallback: Runtime topology request failed. Retry after the backend is reachable again.
runtimeTopologyRuntimeDialogDescription: Define the provider/model route and the execution surface this runtime should expose.
runtimeTopologyAccountDialogDescription: Register one concrete account under a channel type. Use config and metadata JSON for channel-specific fields.
runtimeTopologyBindingDialogDescription: Route one channel account to one runtime. Binding mode must stay consistent for all bindings under the same account.
runtimeTopologyEnabledHint: Disabled entries stay in topology but are excluded from active routing.
runtimeTopologyDisableRuntimeHint: Disabling this runtime immediately makes all related bindings inactive, but keeps the binding records.
runtimeTopologyDisableAccountHint: Disabling this account immediately makes all related bindings inactive, but keeps the binding records.
runtimeTopologyAllowPublicReplyHint: When enabled, this binding may respond in shared/public channel contexts.
runtimeTopologyJsonFieldHint: JSON object only. Keep advanced routing or runtime-specific fields here.
runtimeTopologyJsonObjectRequired: This field must be a JSON object.
runtimeTopologyPriorityInvalid: Priority must be a positive integer.
runtimeTopologyNoEnabledAccountsHint: No enabled channel account is available for an active binding.
runtimeTopologyNoEnabledRuntimesHint: No enabled runtime is available for an active binding.
runtimeTopologyBindingTargetEnabledHint: Active bindings must point to enabled channel accounts and runtimes.
chatNoModelsDescription: Discover models from a provider or add them from the models workspace before starting a chat.
